title: MiFID II Client Suitability — Know-Your-Client Refresh Overdue by Risk Segment

compliance.assurance_rationale: MiFID II Art.25 — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that MiFID II Art.25 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.5.12: MiFID II Client Suitability — Know-Your-Client Refresh Overdue by Risk Segment.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.5.12' running on index=crm sourcetype="kyc:client" (client_id, risk_segment, next_review_date, last_review_date, status), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk DB Connect to CRM/KYC warehouse
spl:
index=crm sourcetype="kyc:client" risk_segment IN ("high","PEP","complex_product_user") earliest=-1d
| eval nrd=strptime(next_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where nrd<now() OR status!="current"
| stats count by risk_segment, status
| sort - count
description: Suitability depends on current client facts. Flagging high-risk clients whose KYC refresh date passed without completion reduces the risk of recommending instruments inconsistent with updated circumstances.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for MiFID II compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes crm and sourcetypes kyc:client. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align segments with internal policy; (2) exclude closed accounts; (3) route to advisory supervision queue; (4) integrate with order blocking flags optionally; (5) document remediation in suitability file—not raw Splunk. Indexes required: `index=crm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=kyc:client`.
visualization: Table (clients at risk), Column chart by segment, Single value (count). Key fields: `nrd`, `risk_segment`, `status`.
knownFalsePositives: Reporting gaps during venue maintenance, scheduled feed restatements, or vendor data corrections.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect to CRM/KYC warehouse.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=crm` `sourcetype="kyc:client"` (client_id, risk_segment, next_review_date, last_review_date, status).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align segments with internal policy; (2) exclude closed accounts; (3) route to advisory supervision queue; (4) integrate with order blocking flags optionally; (5) document remediation in suitability file—not raw Splunk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=crm sourcetype="kyc:client" risk_segment IN ("high","PEP","complex_product_user") earliest=-1d
| eval nrd=strptime(next_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where nrd<now() OR status!="current"
| stats count by risk_segment, status
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**MiFID II Client Suitability — Know-Your-Client Refresh Overdue by Risk Segment** — Suitability depends on current client facts. Flagging high-risk clients whose KYC refresh date passed without completion reduces the risk of recommending instruments inconsistent with updated circumstances.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=crm` `sourcetype="kyc:client"` (client_id, risk_segment, next_review_date, last_review_date, status). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect to CRM/KYC warehouse. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: crm; **sourcetype**: kyc:client. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=crm, sourcetype="kyc:client", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **nrd** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where nrd<now() OR status!="current"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by risk_segment, status** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (clients at risk), Column chart by segment, Single value (count).
